items.title: Microsoft and OpenAI end their exclusive and revenue-sharing deal
items.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have ended their exclusive partnership and revenue-sharing arrangement, signaling a major shift in the AI industry landscape.
items.insight: This move could reshape cloud AI offerings and increase competition. Developers relying on OpenAI via Azure should monitor how this affects API pricing, availability, and integration options.
items.english_title: Microsoft and OpenAI end their exclusive and revenue-sharing deal

items.title: 4TB of voice samples just stolen from 40k AI contractors at Mercor
items.summary: A data breach at Mercor led to the theft of 4TB of voice samples from 40,000 AI contractors, raising serious privacy and security concerns.
items.insight: Voice data is highly sensitive and can be used for deepfakes or identity theft. Companies handling biometric data must implement robust security and comply with privacy regulations.
items.english_title: 4TB of voice samples just stolen from 40k AI contractors at Mercor

items.title: GTFOBins
items.summary: GTFOBins is a curated list of Unix binaries that can be exploited to bypass local security restrictions, useful for penetration testing and system hardening.
items.insight: System administrators and security professionals should be aware of these binaries to audit and secure systems against privilege escalation attacks. The list serves as a practical reference for both defenders and offensive testers.
items.english_title: GTFOBins

items.title: High Performance Git
items.summary: High Performance Git provides tips and techniques to optimize Git operations for large repositories and improve developer workflow efficiency.
items.insight: For teams with massive codebases, slow Git commands can be a bottleneck. Applying these performance strategies can save significant time and reduce frustration for developers.
items.english_title: High Performance Git

items.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
items.summary: DeepMind’s David Silver raises $1.1 billion to develop AI that learns without relying on human-annotated data.
items.insight: If successful, this could dramatically reduce the cost and effort of training AI models, marking a paradigm shift for AI research and engineering.
items.english_title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data

items.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
items.summary: OpenAI is reportedly considering making a phone where AI agents replace traditional apps.
items.insight: If realized, this could fundamentally change mobile computing by shifting from app-based interfaces to agent-driven interactions. Developers and product managers should prepare for a potential paradigm shift in how users engage with software.
items.english_title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps

items.title: Critical infrastructure giant Itron says it was hacked
items.summary: Critical infrastructure giant Itron confirmed it was hacked.
items.insight: This breach underscores persistent risks to critical infrastructure and OT systems. IT professionals in industrial sectors must reassess supply chain security and incident response plans.
items.english_title: Critical infrastructure giant Itron says it was hacked

items.title: Google is testing AI chatbot search for YouTube
items.summary: Google is testing an AI chatbot for searching YouTube.
items.insight: Conversational AI search on YouTube could transform video discovery and user interaction. Developers should consider how such agents might change SEO and content delivery strategies.
items.english_title: Google is testing AI chatbot search for YouTube

items.title: Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux
items.summary: Canonical announced a plan to integrate AI into Ubuntu Linux.
items.insight: This move could simplify AI/ML deployment on Linux, making it easier for developers and sysadmins to run models locally. IT professionals should monitor for native AI tooling in upcoming Ubuntu releases.
items.english_title: Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux

items.title: Pgrx: Build Postgres Extensions with Rust
items.summary: Pgrx is a framework that enables building PostgreSQL extensions using the Rust programming language, combining Rust's safety with database functionality.
items.insight: Using Rust for Postgres extensions allows memory-safe and high-performance database logic. This is valuable for backend developers and DBAs seeking to extend Postgres without C pitfalls.
items.english_title: Pgrx: Build Postgres Extensions with Rust

items.title: WASM is not quite a stack machine
items.summary: An analysis explaining why WebAssembly is not exactly a pure stack machine, delving into its instruction set and execution model nuances.
items.insight: Compiler engineers and WASM runtime developers need to understand these subtleties for correct optimization and code generation. It also affects debugging and performance analysis.
items.english_title: WASM is not quite a stack machine

items.title: Three men are facing charges in Toronto SMS Blaster arrests
items.summary: Three individuals have been charged in Toronto for using SMS blasters to send spam messages, highlighting the persistence of this attack vector.
items.insight: SMS blasting remains a threat for social engineering and spam. Security professionals should educate users and implement SMS filtering or authentication measures.
items.english_title: Three men are facing charges in Toronto SMS Blaster arrests

items.title: LingBot-Map: Streaming 3D reconstruction with geometric context transformer
items.summary: LingBot-Map presents a new method for streaming 3D reconstruction using a geometric context transformer, advancing real-time 3D perception.
items.insight: This research is relevant for autonomous navigation, AR/VR, and robotics. Developers in these fields should watch for practical implementations that enable real-time 3D mapping.
items.english_title: LingBot-Map: Streaming 3D reconstruction with geometric context transformer

items.title: The woes of sanitizing SVGs
items.summary: The article explores the difficulties and best practices for sanitizing SVG files to prevent security vulnerabilities like XSS.
items.insight: Web developers must understand SVG sanitization pitfalls to build secure applications that handle user-uploaded content safely.
items.english_title: The woes of sanitizing SVGs

items.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
items.summary: OpenAI resolves a legal dispute with Microsoft related to its $50 billion Amazon deal.
items.insight: This legal resolution clarifies relationships among AI leaders and cloud providers, affecting enterprise decisions on AI infrastructure and partnerships.
items.english_title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal

items.title: Data center demand drives 66% surge in natural gas power plant costs
items.summary: Rising data center energy demand drives a 66% increase in natural gas power plant costs.
items.insight: IT infrastructure planners must factor in escalating energy costs and environmental impacts when designing and scaling data centers, pushing for greener solutions.
items.english_title: Data center demand drives 66% surge in natural gas power plant costs

items.title: Google employees ask Sundar Pichai to say no to classified military AI use
items.summary: Google employees urged CEO Sundar Pichai to refuse classified military AI projects.
items.insight: This internal pressure reflects the growing ethical debate around military AI. IT professionals should consider how such policies may affect their own work and company reputation.
items.english_title: Google employees ask Sundar Pichai to say no to classified military AI use

items.title: Scraping 241 UK council planning portals – 2.6M decisions so far
items.summary: A project scraped 2.6 million planning decisions from 241 UK council websites, demonstrating large-scale data collection from diverse portals.
items.insight: This showcases web scraping at scale, including handling inconsistencies across portals. Developers can learn techniques for aggregating open data and dealing with anti-scraping measures.
items.english_title: Scraping 241 UK council planning portals – 2.6M decisions so far

items.title: How I leared what a decoupling capacitor is for, the hard way
items.summary: A personal account of learning the hard way why decoupling capacitors are critical in circuit design, due to a hardware failure.
items.insight: Decoupling capacitors are essential for stable power delivery in digital circuits. This anecdote reinforces best practices for hardware engineers and hobbyists.
items.english_title: How I leared what a decoupling capacitor is for, the hard way

items.title: The quiet resurgence of RF engineering
items.summary: A look at the growing importance of radio frequency engineering in modern wireless technologies like IoT, 5G, and satellite communications.
items.insight: As wireless technologies expand, RF skills are increasingly in demand. Networking and hardware professionals should brush up on RF principles to design and troubleshoot modern systems.
items.english_title: The quiet resurgence of RF engineering

items.title: Easyduino: Open Source PCB Devboards for KiCad
items.summary: Easyduino presents open-source PCB development boards designed for use with KiCad.
items.insight: For hardware engineers and hobbyists using KiCad, these open-source boards could accelerate prototyping and foster community-driven design improvements.
items.english_title: Easyduino: Open Source PCB Devboards for KiCad

items.title: Consumers lost $2.1B to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports
items.summary: The FTC reports that consumers lost $2.1 billion to social media scams in 2025.
items.insight: This underscores the importance of cybersecurity awareness and the need for robust fraud detection technologies, which IT professionals should prioritize in system designs.
items.english_title: Consumers lost $2.1B to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports

items.title: What’s behind Europe’s efforts to ditch US software in favor of sovereign tech
items.summary: Europe pursues sovereign technology alternatives to reduce reliance on US software.
items.insight: IT professionals in Europe should monitor regulatory shifts that may mandate or incentivize the use of local tech stacks, affecting software procurement and compliance strategies.
items.english_title: What’s behind Europe’s efforts to ditch US software in favor of sovereign tech

items.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
items.summary: China blocked Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Manus after a months-long probe.
items.insight: This deal highlights growing geopolitical tensions in tech acquisition and talent movement. IT professionals should monitor how such blocks affect access to emerging AI technologies and cross-border collaboration.
items.english_title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe

items.title: Mo RAM, Mo Problems (2025)
items.summary: A discussion of the problems that arise with large amounts of RAM, including memory management, fragmentation, and software design challenges.
items.insight: Even with abundant memory, inefficiencies in allocation and access patterns can degrade performance. IT pros should still optimize memory usage and consider NUMA effects in high-RAM systems.
items.english_title: Mo RAM, Mo Problems (2025)

items.title: Networking changes coming in macOS 27
items.summary: Upcoming networking changes in macOS 27 are detailed, potentially affecting system administrators and developers.
items.insight: IT professionals managing Mac environments should stay informed to ensure compatibility and adjust network configurations accordingly.
items.english_title: Networking changes coming in macOS 27

items.title: Hacker who allegedly carried out cyberattacks for China is extradited to US
items.summary: A hacker accused of conducting cyberattacks on behalf of China is extradited to the United States.
items.insight: This event highlights ongoing state-sponsored cyber threats, reminding IT security teams to stay vigilant against advanced persistent threats and geopolitical risks.
items.english_title: Hacker who allegedly carried out cyberattacks for China is extradited to US

items.title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space
items.summary: Meta signed a deal to receive solar power at night beamed from space.
items.insight: This novel energy solution could influence how tech companies power their data centers. IT operations teams should watch for scalable space-based solar as a potential future energy source.
items.english_title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space

items.title: Talkie: a 13B vintage language model from 1930
items.summary: Talkie describes a 13-billion parameter language model built with vintage 1930s technology, blending retro hardware with modern AI concepts.
items.insight: While mostly a thought experiment or artistic project, it highlights the long history of ideas behind large language models. IT professionals might find it inspiring but not directly actionable.
items.english_title: Talkie: a 13B vintage language model from 1930

items.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
items.summary: Skye's AI-powered home screen app for iPhone receives investor backing ahead of its launch.
items.insight: This signals growing interest in AI-enhanced mobile interfaces, which may influence how IT professionals design and optimize user experiences for mobile apps.
items.english_title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
